content:
After the Lunar New Year in 2026, I’d like to record the second time I’ve taken my puppy back to my hometown and what has been happening in my life. When I got off work on February 13, I didn’t go back to my hometown immediately. I wanted to clean my house, and I had an English class on Saturday, so I wanted to finish everything before going back to my hometown.
On Sunday, my mom and a friend drove to Taipei, and I took them to Ootoya for lunch and walked around the area afterward.
We went back to my house, and then I started packing my things and my puppy’s things, getting ready to go to my hometown. Before that, my puppy had gotten motion sickness last year, so my mom prepared some motion sickness medicine for her. Fortunately, my puppy didn’t get carsick this time. She’s really my best daughter.
We arrived in my hometown at about 7:00 p.m. I unpacked and got some water for my puppy, and then we had lamb hot pot together for dinner. Lamb hot pot is a traditional specialty from my hometown, so this time I took it back to Taipei to share with my friends.
My puppy was still timid this time because she was afraid of my mom’s puppies. When we had dinner, she would hide behind me every time. However, I thought she was so cute.
After dinner, I took my puppy to my room and prepared dinner for her. Then I found my CDs and started ripping all of them to FLAC format and listening to them using Roon.
During the process, I found many songs that I had not listened to for a long time. They brought back many memories, so I decided to keep them as well.
My puppy and I sleplt together every night. My puppy looked so cute sleeping on the bed. Before I knew it, having her sleep next to me made me feel calm and safe.
I was with my family every day until I came back to Taipei. Actually, I also did a lot of things during this holiday, but above all, these are my most precious memories.
title: Lunar New Year in 2026

content:
Debian is my favorite Linux distribution. I installed it on my retired PC and use it as my server OS. I have used Debian for over a decade. It is free, open, and very stable. I use it to practice build many server, such as DNS, NAT, SMB, Website, Database, and so on.
In 2026, I will rewrite an article documenting my Debian usage. The first section will describe the process of installing Debian, and then I will write more articles about how to use Debian to set up various types of servers.
Now, I’m going to show you how to install Debian with VirtualBox. Here we go!
We need to get the Debian installation media from the official website first. I am providing the link, and you can click it to download.
After downloading the installation media, open VirtualBox. We need to create a virtual machine. At this step, we do not need “Proceed with Unattended Installation,” so uncheck the box.
Next, configure the virtual hardware. I recommend increasing the disk size because we will need enough space to set up various types of servers.
Click Finish to complete the creation of the virtual machine.
Now everything is ready, and we can begin installing Debian.
Click Start to power on the virtual machine, then select Install because I prefer using the text‑based installer.
Select a language, and choose English.
Select your location, and choose Taiwan.
Configure locales, and choose United States.
Configure the keyboard, and choose American English.
Configure the network, then enter a hostname.
Configure the network again, then either leave the domain name blank or enter your own.
Enter the root password.
Re‑enter the root password.
Enter your full name.
Enter your username.
Enter the password for your account.
Re‑enter the password.
Choose Guided – use entire disk.
Press Enter to continue.
Choose All files in one partition (recommended for new users).
Choose Finish partitioning and write changes to disk.
Choose Yes.
Choose No when asked to scan extra installation media.
Choose Yes to use a network mirror.
Choose Taiwan.
Choose any mirror you prefer.
Leave the proxy field blank.
For popularity‑contest, choose No.
For software selection, choose SSH Server and standard system utilities.
For GRUB installation, choose Yes.
Select /dev/sda.
Choose Continue to reboot.
Now you have a working Debian virtual machine.
I will share more Debian‑related articles in the future.
title: Installing Debian Linux Distribution

content:
Oh my God. I cannot believe that my first article is about my broken NAS in 2026. When I was still living in my hometown, I bought a Synology NAS. Synology NAS devices are very convenient and stable. They offer many applications that allow me to access my documents and photos, and even build virtual machines from anywhere. After that, I canceled all of my cloud storage subscriptions.
Back then, I bought a 4‑bay NAS and used RAID 5 to protect my data. If one hard disk failed, I could simply replace it and the system would automatically rebuild the data. But for the first time, the failure wasn’t the hard disk — it was the machine itself. So I just ended up buying a new one.
Fortunately, I’m used to setting up servers with my retired PCs, and I also have scheduled backups for my NAS data. When I found it was broken, I immediately backed up my backup NAS data from the server to another hard disk, and then I bought a new one. This time, I decided to go with a 2‑bay NAS because I don’t need that much storage space.
Now all my NAS data has been recovered, and the new NAS is running very smoothly. I can use it just like before, and I hope it will last for a long time.
title: My Broken NAS in 2026

content:
After I left IBM, the first thing I wanted to do was get my life back. So I decided to get a puppy. I started looking on different websites, and eventually I found one on 乖乖狗寵物家.
When I first saw her, I felt that she was incredibly adorable, and she had a very special fur color. I knew it was her at that moment. I immediately sent a message to the shop and made a reservation to visit her. I wanted to take her home as soon as possible.
That day, I really looked forward to seeing her. When it was time, I took an Uber, feeling very happy. After about 30 minutes, I arrived at the shop. As soon as I went in, the owner put her on the ground. She was full of energy and kept following me everywhere, even chewing on my pants. It seemed like she knew I was there to take her home. I knew she was the best encounter of my life. Then I started handling the paperwork. However, I didn’t have any dog supplies at home. Fortunately, the shop also sold some basic pet supplies, so I bought them there right away.
When we got home, I opened the box and let her see her new home. Then I started setting up her space. After that, I put her in her place, and I was very surprised because she seemed to know this was her home. She immediately wanted to explore everywhere in the house.
She fit in very well in her new home. After playing for a while, she fell asleep comfortably in her place. The place was temporary, and I planned to prepare a better one for her.
In the end, I took her home, which was a lifelong promise I made to her. I was truly grateful that she became part of my life. From that moment on, we began our journey together.
title: Welcome to My Family
